log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable with a size of 37,400 bytes, and it has been run in CAPA, VirusTotal Cuckoofork, and VirusTotal Jujubox sandboxes. The tags suggest it might be a spreader with runtime
The sample has dropped two files, created one process, and has a low number of unique IPs and domains. There are no registry persistence keys or highlighted API calls.
The process created by the sample is a copy of itself, and it loads several system DLLs, including rasman.dll, which is related to the RASMAN service opened by the sample.
The sample does not create any registry persistence keys, which suggests that it may not be designed to persist on the system after the initial execution.
The sample establishes connections to two IP addresses, 192.99.43.80 and 204.11.56.48, which are associated with the domains bulkbacklinks.com and groupesorepco.com, respectively.
The sample drops two files, hummy.exe and a copy of itself in the AppData/Local/Temp directory.
The sample exhibits various capabilities, including creating or opening files, creating processes, getting HTTP content length, and sending HTTP requests. It also contains anti-VM strings, suggesting
